page: Microsoft Azure F4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the F-series, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. This configuration provides 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and a 50 MB L3 cache. It supports AVX, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities.

The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. For local storage, a 66 GB temporary disk is bundled with the server. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in the base configuration. This hardware profile represents a lower RAM-to-CPU ratio, positioning the server for compute-focused workloads that do not require massive memory footprints.

In benchmark evaluations, the F4 demonstrates modest performance characteristics. Its single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests falls within the bottom 25% of tested servers, with Geekbench single-core scores landing in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached read and write speeds categorized in the bottom 25%. However, the server achieves average performance in specific tasks, including single-threaded gzip compression, PassMark CPU extended instructions, PassMark database operations, and small-model (135M) LLM inference prompt processing and text generation.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the F4 offers a dedicated compute resource allocation at a lower hardware tier, making it suitable for entry-level compute tasks where shared-core burstable instances are undesirable due to performance unpredictability. Given its balanced but modest CPU capabilities, 8.0 GB of RAM, and local storage, this server is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, software compilation tasks with moderate resource demands, and small-scale utility scripting.
description: Microsoft Azure F4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and includes 66 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance supports AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets. Benchmark data indicates modest overall performance, with CPU and memory bandwidth results generally falling in the bottom 25%, though it achieves average marks in database operations, gzip compression, and small-model LLM inference. Qualitatively, the F4 offers a dedicated, budget-friendly compute option for workloads requiring predictable CPU allocation without high memory demands. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database tasks, and moderate software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 66 GB of local storage, designed for entry-level dedicated compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F4 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 66 GB of local storage for compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated four-core compute instance with balanced local storage and memory for entry-level processing and utility workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 physical cores at 2.3 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per vCPU)
Average performance in database operations and small-model LLM inference
Suitable for lightweight web serving, basic databases, and utility scripting
